mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in retail sales signals expanding consumer demand, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year rank correlation between RSAFS month-over-month growth and 20-day forward returns is negative or below 0.05; or positive returns are not significantly higher in months with above-median RSAFS growth (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0% signals labor market deterioration, reducing consumer spending power and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In walk-forward, the median 60-day forward return following UNRATE > 4.0% is not negative, or the fraction of negative forward returns is less than 55% across all triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly declines in housing starts and industrial production signal synchronized contraction in construction and manufacturing, deeply negative for economic output and S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Median 60-day forward return after both HOUST and INDPRO decline month-on-month is not negative, or the binary signal has rank IC of absolute value < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS rising above 3.0%), a decline in housing starts predicts negative forward returns over 60 days because tighter policy directly impacts interest-rate-sensitive sectors; during easing cycles the effect is muted or positive.
failure_condition: In regimes where FEDFUNDS > 3.0%, the rank IC of HOUST month-over-month change on 60-day forward returns is not negative (or |IC| < 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% indicates a sudden supply-side disruption, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days due to earnings downgrades and uncertainty.
failure_condition: In walk-forward, the 5-day forward return after the trigger is not negative in at least 60% of occurrences, or the mean return is not statistically different from zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is low (<4.0%) and payroll employment growth exceeds 200k per month, the labor market is overheating, signaling wage inflation and imminent Fed tightening, which depresses S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Median 20-day forward return following the joint condition is not negative, or the fraction of negative returns is less than 55% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: The level of the federal funds rate inversely proxies the discount rate applied to equity cash flows: a lower fed funds rate implies higher present value of future earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days, and vice versa.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year correlation between FEDFUNDS level and 5-day forward returns is positive (above +0.05), or the rank IC over the full sample is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In economic expansions (GDPC1 annualized quarterly growth > 2.5%), rising retail sales confirm demand momentum and support positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days; in contractions the signal is less reliable or reverses.
failure_condition: In quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5%, the rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month growth on 20-day forward returns is not positive (>0.05) or the forward return distribution is not significantly above zero (one-sample t-test p > 0.10).
